title: Requirements, ER Modeling, Functional Dependencies, and Normalization · Query Repair Bay
summary: A growing incident system repeats team and member facts, mixes multiple values per cell, and produces update anomalies. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a normalized incident-system schema defense and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence de…
objectives: Derive entities, attributes, identifiers, relationships, optionality, and business rules from stakeholder language.
Model one-to-one, one-to-many, and many-to-many relationships with minimum and maximum cardinality.
Identify functional dependencies and candidate keys from semantics rather than sample coincidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Lossless Dependency Design
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-lossless-dependency-design-bgx3rq" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; schema grain, relationship, dependency, or decomposition evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

-- Evidence: sql-lossless-dependency-design-bgx3rq
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
CREATE TABLE model_status_history_bgx3rq (
  ticket_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES tickets(ticket_id),
  changed_at TEXT NOT NULL,
  status TEXT NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (ticket_id, changed_at)
);
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
